title: Multi-Agent Ecosystem for Strategic Task Reasoning and Orchestration

coordinator.department: Department of Computer Science, Chair of AI Foundations

abstract: MAESTRO develops next-generation AI agent architectures that combine structured planning, persistent memory management, robust multi-step reasoning, and principled integration of external tools and APIs to autonomously accomplish complex real-world tasks across the Apply AI sectors. The project advances the state of the art in three tightly integrated research pillars unified by a cross-cutting External Tool and API Orchestration Layer: (1) neuro-symbolic planning and reasoning engines that decompose high-level goals into executable sub-task graphs with verifiable intermediate states; (2) adaptive memory architectures that unify short-term working memory with external long-term knowledge stores to sustain coherent, context-aware agent behaviour over extended interactions; and (3) decentralised multi-agent coordination protocols that enable heterogeneous AI agents to negotiate, delegate, and jointly execute tasks with provable convergence guarantees. The External Tool and API Orchestration Layer provides a typed tool registry, formal tool selection and invocation policies, standardised API adapters, post-invocation verification, execution monitoring with fallback/recovery, and auditable tool execution traces — addressing the essential requirement for external tool and API integration to overcome limitations of large AI models. MAESTRO validates these capabilities through two call-aligned Apply AI sector demonstrators — healthcare clinical decision support and advanced manufacturing process optimisation — plus a cross-sector logistics and supply chain transfer demonstrator that confirms the architecture's portability beyond the minimum sector requirement. All demonstrators are co-designed with sector-leading industry partners, incorporate quantitative KPIs and benchmarking, and are validated in operational environments. All results will be made available through the AI-on-demand platform and validated in Testing and Experiment Facilities, contributing directly to the Apply AI Strategy and the European Partnership on AI, Data and Robotics (ADRA).
